fields.description:
## Why

`reconciler.baseline_dual_write` and `reconciler.baseline_consumer_swap` are the
shadow/consume phase flags of the convergence rollout (epic 3006-e198 / story a118).
Both are now flipped ON in this project and have run clean in production, so the
phased switches have served their purpose. Retire them and hardcode always-on
(baseline always dual-written AND always consumed as the outbound field-differ's
arbitration ancestor).

## What

- Remove both fields + validators from `ReconcilerConfig` (`_config_schema.py:399,403`
  and parser `:533,534`).
- Hardcode always-consume: `outbound_fields.py:418-419` unconditionally reads
  `binding_store.get_baseline(local_id)` as `arbitration_prev`; collapse the boolean
  threading through `run_differs.py:458/473`, `outbound_differ.py:374/626/731/805`.
- Hardcode always-dual-write at `reconcile.py:938-954`. **`baseline_shadow.py` does TWO
  things — do NOT delete it wholesale.** `run_dual_write_shadow` (1) ADVANCES per-binding
  baselines via `binding_store.set_baseline(local_id, curr_snapshot[jira_key])`
  (baseline_shadow.py:84) — STILL NEEDED after hardcoding always-on — and (2) runs the
  equivalence check (`_mirrored_equal`, divergent counting, the
  `RECON: baseline_shadow_check` stderr line) — the rollout gate, now permanently
  satisfied and dead. First EXTRACT the `set_baseline` advancement loop into a standalone
  helper (e.g. `reconcile._advance_baselines`) called UNCONDITIONALLY before `save()`; then
  delete ONLY the equivalence-check portions (`_mirrored_equal`, divergence counting, the
  `baseline_shadow_check`/`baseline_shadow_divergence` diagnostics). If that leaves
  `baseline_shadow.py` empty, remove the file; otherwise keep the advancement helper.
- Remove the two lines from `rebar.toml` (`:65`, `:70`).
- Do NOT delete `prev_snapshot`/`prev_jira_fields` — still needed to advance/seed baselines.

## Scope

Backward-compat verdict: SAFE. Baseline is persisted additively into `bindings.json`
(no store-version bump; older binaries ignore the extra keys, per ADR-0026), the
reconciler is a single advisory-locked GHA host process (no old-clone race), and
neither flag changes the shape of anything written to Jira or the tickets branch.
Out of scope: the KEEP decisions (signature mirror, completion floor).

## Cold-start note (address, don't skip)

`seed_baselines_from_snapshot()` exists but is never called, so a repo upgrading from
a version that never dual-wrote gets `get_baseline()==None` → "local-wins" until
baselines populate lazily (could silently revert a concurrent Jira edit on first
passes). Chosen resolution (lower-risk than wiring seeding into the hot path):
**document the one-pass warm-up window AND make it observable** — the reconciler
emits a `RECON: baseline_cold_start local_id=<id>` line to stderr whenever
`get_baseline()==None` for a confirmed binding, so operators can identify the window
from GHA run logs, and `docs/jira-sync-setup.md` describes the warm-up window and how
baselines populate lazily on the first always-on passes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `baseline_dual_write` + `baseline_consumer_swap` removed from schema, parser, and `rebar.toml`; each is now an unknown key (a `[reconciler]` config setting either name is warned-and-ignored, per the removed-key backcompat test).
- [ ] Baseline dual-write and consume are unconditional; the boolean threading through `run_differs.py` / `outbound_differ.py` / `outbound_fields.py` is collapsed; no dead back-out branch remains.
- [ ] Dead shadow machinery (`baseline_shadow.py`) removed if fully unreachable, or a comment records why it stays.
- [ ] Cold-start local-wins window is made observable and documented: the reconciler emits a `RECON: baseline_cold_start local_id=<id>` stderr line whenever `get_baseline()==None` for a confirmed binding, and `docs/jira-sync-setup.md` documents the one-pass warm-up window. (A unit test asserts the log line fires on a cold-start binding.)
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.
- [ ] Reconcile dry-run behavior is proven unchanged by a NEW test that pins the one arbitration decision the removed flags controlled and would FLIP if that behavior regressed (not a pre-existing test merely staying green). Because the flags were already `true` in prod, the hardcoded behavior must equal the flags-true behavior; and since the flags are now removed, the flags-true tree can no longer be run to capture a literal stdout baseline — so the proof is a targeted decision-pin rather than a full-stdout diff (equivalent invariant, non-flaky): with a baseline present that DIFFERS from a STALE `prev_jira_fields`, the outbound dry-run must SUPPRESS the field (baseline consulted as `arbitration_prev`); reverting to prev_snapshot arbitration flips the decision and fails the test. Delivered by `test_dryrun_arbitration_pinned_to_baseline` in `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_baseline_arbitration.py` (verified to flip on revert).
